You need to accomplish two things in this phase:
1. Profile the uploaded raw CSV files to understand their structure, column semantics, data quality, and potential join keys across datasets.
2. Use that profile to generate a complete Bronze STTM CSV that covers every column with ingestion rules (renaming, type casting, metadata injection).

Think about what the business intent implies about which columns matter most. Plan which tools to call and in what order. Verify each output before proceeding.

args.goal: Profile the uploaded raw CSV files (sales_data.csv, products.csv, stores.csv) to understand their structure, column semantics, data quality, and potential join keys. The business intent is to calculate total sales for all products, so pay close attention to sales amounts, product identifiers, and any related columns that could link products to sales. The output profile will be used to generate the Bronze STTM.

Generate a complete Bronze STTM CSV based on the previously generated data profile. The STTM should include ingestion rules (renaming, type casting, metadata injection) for all columns across all uploaded raw CSV files (sales_data.csv, products.csv, stores.csv). The business intent is to calculate total sales for all products, so ensure that sales figures, product identifiers, and any related attributes are correctly mapped and typed for downstream use. The Bronze STTM will be used to ingest the raw data into the Bronze layer.
